plainEnglish: At Level AAA, if an authenticated session expires while a user is working, they can log back in and continue without losing any data. Preserve form input and state across re-authentication so a timeout doesn't destroy someone's work.
whyItMatters: Users who work slowly due to a disability are the most likely to hit a session timeout and lose everything they entered if data isn't preserved across re-login.
quickCheck: Let a session expire mid-form, log back in, and confirm your entered data is still there.
commonMistakes: Discarding form data when the session expires instead of restoring it after re-login.
Redirecting expired sessions to a login page that loses the user's place and input.
Showing a session-expired error that forces a full restart of a multi-step form.

codeExamples.label: Persist the draft so it survives re-authentication
Redirect on expiry, losing all in-memory state
codeExamples.code:
// On 401, redirect to login and lose all unsaved form state
if (res.status === 401) window.location.href = "/login";
